task: I need to leave a note for the next person about a room that still needs attention, and I want it to be easy to read quickly during a busy shift. Can you help me put the details in a clear order so it’s simple to follow later?

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — here’s a simple order that’s easy to scan fast:

**Room #:**  
**Date/Time:**  
**Issue:**  
**Dirty / Wet / Broken / Missing:**  
**Already done:**  
**Next person / follow-up needed:**  

If you want, I can also turn this into a **short fill-in template** you can copy and use on every shift.

user_simulator.text:
Yeah, that’s the right idea. I’d want it a little more work-ready though — something that tells the next person what matters first, in the order they need it.

Maybe like:

Room #:
What’s wrong:
What I already did:
What still needs to be done:
Anything to watch for:

That’s easier for me to scan fast on a busy hall. If you want, make it into a short fill-in note I can just copy each time.

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — here’s a short, work-ready fill-in note you can copy fast:

**Room #:**  
**What’s wrong:**  
**What I already did:**  
**What still needs to be done:**  
**Anything to watch for:**  

If you want, I can also make it even shorter for super-fast shift notes.

user_simulator.text: Yeah, that works. Short and plain is better for this kind of thing. I can use that.
